{{Shrine |name = Ōgo Shrine |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == {{神社 |名称 = 大胡神社 |画像 = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[河原浜町]]615 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大胡神社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[豊城入彦命]] |神体 = |社格 =旧郷社 |創建 =不詳 |本殿 = |別名 =近戸大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = 太々神楽 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=大胡神社|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''大胡神社'''（おおごじんじゃ）は[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[河原浜町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。かつては南に位置する[[大胡城]]の鎮守であり、[[明治|明治時代]]までは'''近戸神社'''と称した。 == 歴史 == [[赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|三夜沢赤城神社]]より分祀されたと伝わるが、その年代は不詳{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 社伝によると当社の南にある大胡城内に[[二宮赤城神社]]の別当寺の玉蔵院があった{{Cite web|和書|title=III 赤城山信仰と南麓集落にみる歴史的風致|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/59/rekimachi_2shou_3.pdf|website=前橋市|accessdate=2025-02-04}}。 二宮赤城神社から三夜沢赤城神社へ神体が往復する御神幸では休憩地となる{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 [[天正]]年間に大胡城主[[大胡高繁|大胡常陸介高繁]]が信仰し、天正17年（[[1589年]]）に高繁は三夜沢赤城神社神官・奈良原紀伊守に宛てて大胡城鎮守の近戸神社奉祭のために奈良原父子のいずれかが来て祀ってほしい旨の書状を出している。これ以降奈良原氏が奉斎している{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 明治42年（[[1909年]]）6月に近戸大明神を改めて大胡神社と称した{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 明治42年（1909年）6月5日に[[勢多郡]][[大胡町]]内の[[大胡町 (前橋市)|大胡町]]村社・菅原神社、[[茂木町 (前橋市)|茂木村]]村社・熱田神社、[[堀越町 (前橋市)|堀越村]]村社・諏訪神社、[[滝窪町|滝窪村]]村社・八柱神社、[[横沢町 (前橋市)|横沢村]]村社・赤城神社、[[樋越町|樋越村]]村社・八坂神社、[[上大屋町|上大屋村]]村社・八坂神社などを合祀。明治43年（[[1910年]]）8月12日に勢多郡[[桂萱村]]の[[堀之下町|堀ノ下村]]村社・熊野神社などを合祀{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 [[昭和]]18年（[[1943年]]）に東からの参道を南向きに付け替えた{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 昭和19年（[[1944年]]）4月26日、郷社となった{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}。 == 境内 == 拝殿の向かって右に神楽殿があり、毎年神楽が奉納される。 大胡町大胡の出身で、公選初代の大胡町長を務めた後、[[群馬県議会]]議員に5期連続当選、県議会議長を務めた[[岡田義正]]の顕彰碑がある{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=433-435}}。 == 祭神 == * [[大国主|大己貴命]] * [[豊城入彦命]] * ほか43柱 * 相殿8柱 {{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == 祭礼 == === 太々神楽（前橋市重要無形民俗文化財） === 境内の神楽殿で毎年4月10日の祭日に奉納される豊作祈願の[[神楽]]{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|p=1179}}。足軽町地区の太々神楽保存会によって奉納される。十三座の神楽が舞われる。かつては河原浜地区と足軽町地区で交互に奉納していたが、現在では足軽町だけとなっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=大胡エリアの伝統芸能など|publisher=前橋市|accessdate=2020-05-31}}。 === 大胡祇園まつり === 毎年7月最終の土曜・日曜2日間で行われる[[祇園祭|祭]]。八坂神社（大胡町）の祭礼だが、神体の幣束は大胡神社から与えられる{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1032-1234}}。 {{Main|大胡祇園まつり}} == 文化財 == === [[前橋市指定文化財一覧|前橋市指定文化財]] === 前橋市指定重要文化財 * 大胡神社の[[算額]]（平成6年4月5日指定）{{Cite web |title=市指定文化財一覧{{!}}前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23 |language=ja}} - [[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）に大原福太郎が奉納したもの。大原福太郎の[[和算]]の師は[[船津伝次平|船津正武（伝次平）]]であるという。大正時代の和算の算学は全国的にも珍しい{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=346-351}}。 前橋市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 大胡神社太々神楽（昭和44年7月7日指定） 前橋市指定天然記念物 * 大胡神社の[[ムクロジ]]（平成20年3月19日） - 推定[[樹齢]]300年以上、目通り周3.7メートル、高さ25メートル{{Cite book|和書 |first=克典 |last=梅澤 |first2=良久 |last2=木暮 |first3=晃 |last3=真下 |first4=雅順 |last4=近藤 |first5=史人 |last5=池田 |first6=綾子 |last6=綿貫 |first7=亮介 |last7=清水 |title=年報 : 文化財調査報告書 |origdate=2008-12-12 |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128 |ncid=AA12252855 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.16128 |volume=38 |publisher= |pages=6-7}}。 == 所在地 == * [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[河原浜町]]615 == アクセス == * [[北関東自動車道]][[駒形インターチェンジ|駒形IC]]より約20分 * [[上毛電気鉄道]][[大胡駅]]より徒歩25分 == 周辺 == * [[大胡城]]址 * [[群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|群馬県道16号]] * [[荒砥川]] * [[養林寺 (前橋市)|養林寺]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=大胡町誌 |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=大胡町役場 |ref=harv |editor=大胡町誌編纂委員会 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.99955 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955 |ncid=BN03614004}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 大胡神社 (前橋まるごとガイド)] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおこしんしや}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前橋市の建築物]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:赤城神社]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

'''大胡神社'''（おおごじんじゃ）は[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[河原浜町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。かつては南に位置する[[大胡城]]の鎮守であり、[[明治|明治時代]]中期までは'''近戸神社'''と称した。 == 歴史 == [[赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|三夜沢赤城神社]]より分祀されたと伝わるが、その年代は不詳{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 社伝によると当社の南にある大胡城内に[[二宮赤城神社]]の別当寺の玉蔵院があり、当初は二宮系統の近戸神社だったとみられる{{Cite web|和書|title=III 赤城山信仰と南麓集落にみる歴史的風致|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/59/rekimachi_2shou_3.pdf|website=前橋市|accessdate=2025-02-04}}。[[天正]]年間に大胡城主[[大胡高繁|大胡常陸介高繁]]が信仰し、天正17年（[[1589年]]）に高繁は三夜沢赤城神社神官・奈良原紀伊守に宛てて大胡城鎮守の近戸神社奉祭のために奈良原父子のいずれかが来て祀ってほしい旨の書状を出している。これ以降奈良原氏が奉斎している{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。具体的には「三夜沢赤城神社奈良原紀伊守宛の書状」で大胡城主の大胡常陸之介高繁が三夜沢赤城神社の神官に、城の守護として赤城明神を祀りたいと申し出ており、ここから三夜沢系統の近戸神社に変化したとみられる。 二宮赤城神社から三夜沢赤城神社へ神体が往復する御神幸では休憩地となる{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 明治42年（[[1909年]]）6月に近戸大明神を改めて大胡神社と称した{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 明治42年（1909年）6月5日に[[勢多郡]][[大胡町]]内の[[大胡町 (前橋市)|大胡町]]村社・菅原神社、[[茂木町 (前橋市)|茂木村]]村社・熱田神社、[[堀越町 (前橋市)|堀越村]]村社・諏訪神社、[[滝窪町|滝窪村]]村社・八柱神社、[[横沢町 (前橋市)|横沢村]]村社・赤城神社、[[樋越町|樋越村]]村社・八坂神社、[[上大屋町|上大屋村]]村社・八坂神社などを合祀。明治43年（[[1910年]]）8月12日に勢多郡[[桂萱村]]の[[堀之下町|堀ノ下村]]村社・熊野神社などを合祀{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 [[昭和]]18年（[[1943年]]）に東からの参道を南向きに付け替えた{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}}。 昭和19年（[[1944年]]）4月26日、郷社となった{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}。 == 境内 == 拝殿の向かって右に神楽殿があり、毎年神楽が奉納される。 大胡町大胡の出身で、公選初代の大胡町長を務めた後、[[群馬県議会]]議員に5期連続当選、県議会議長を務めた[[岡田義正]]の顕彰碑がある{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=433-435}}。 == 祭神 == * [[大国主|大己貴命]] * [[豊城入彦命]] * ほか43柱 * 相殿8柱 {{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == 祭礼 == === 太々神楽（前橋市重要無形民俗文化財） === 境内の神楽殿で毎年4月10日の祭日に奉納される豊作祈願の[[神楽]]{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|p=1179}}。足軽町地区の太々神楽保存会によって奉納される。十三座の神楽が舞われる。かつては河原浜地区と足軽町地区で交互に奉納していたが、現在では足軽町だけとなっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=大胡エリアの伝統芸能など|publisher=前橋市|accessdate=2020-05-31}}。 === 大胡祇園まつり === 毎年7月最終の土曜・日曜2日間で行われる[[祇園祭|祭]]。八坂神社（大胡町）の祭礼だが、神体の幣束は大胡神社から与えられる{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=1032-1234}}。 {{Main|大胡祇園まつり}} == 文化財 == === [[前橋市指定文化財一覧|前橋市指定文化財]] === 前橋市指定重要文化財 * 大胡神社の[[算額]]（平成6年4月5日指定）{{Cite web |title=市指定文化財一覧{{!}}前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23 |language=ja}} - [[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）に大原福太郎が奉納したもの。大原福太郎の[[和算]]の師は[[船津伝次平|船津正武（伝次平）]]であるという。大正時代の和算の算学は全国的にも珍しい{{Sfn|大胡町誌編纂委員会|1976|pp=346-351}}。 前橋市指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 大胡神社太々神楽（昭和44年7月7日指定） 前橋市指定天然記念物 * 大胡神社の[[ムクロジ]]（平成20年3月19日） - 推定[[樹齢]]300年以上、目通り周3.7メートル、高さ25メートル{{Cite book|和書 |first=克典 |last=梅澤 |first2=良久 |last2=木暮 |first3=晃 |last3=真下 |first4=雅順 |last4=近藤 |first5=史人 |last5=池田 |first6=綾子 |last6=綿貫 |first7=亮介 |last7=清水 |title=年報 : 文化財調査報告書 |origdate=2008-12-12 |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128 |ncid=AA12252855 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.16128 |volume=38 |publisher= |pages=6-7}}。 == 所在地 == * [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[河原浜町]]615 == アクセス == * [[北関東自動車道]][[駒形インターチェンジ|駒形IC]]より約20分 * [[上毛電気鉄道]][[大胡駅]]より徒歩25分 == 周辺 == * [[大胡城]]址 * [[群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|群馬県道16号]] * [[荒砥川]] * [[養林寺 (前橋市)|養林寺]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=大胡町誌 |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=大胡町役場 |ref=harv |editor=大胡町誌編纂委員会 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.99955 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955 |ncid=BN03614004}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 大胡神社 (前橋まるごとガイド)] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおこしんしや}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前橋市の建築物]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:赤城神社]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Shrine |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == in Gumma Prefecture]] [[Category:Oogo Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oogo Shrine]][[Category:History of Maebashi City]] [[Category:Oogo Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] [[Category:Oogo Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] {{Shrine |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] [[Category:Oogo Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Oogo Jinja.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the worship hall |location = {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|18.2|N|139|09|33.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōgo Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} |ShrineRank = Former Gōsha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō Period|ja|天正}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the approach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|pp=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gumma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} {{ill|Maebashi City|ja|前橋市}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kitakantō Expressway|ja|北関東自動車道}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|ja|大胡駅}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} ==
