{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|qid=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|qq=|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from Prefectural Route 16|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|qq=|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|qq=|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] }} [[ja:大胡神社]] Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}}

[[ja:大胡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] }} [[ja:大胡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:大胡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato [[ja:大胡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] }} [[ja:大胡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大胡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438549]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gumma Prefecture]] [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi City]] Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the [[:en:Kita-Kantō Expressway|Kitakantō Expressway]] * 25-minute walk from [[:en:Ōgo Station|Ōgo Station]] on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大胡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kitakantō Expressway}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}} {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kitakantō Expressway}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大胡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kitakantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja|養林寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrin-ji Temple (Maebashi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Ōgo Town History |date=1976-6-15 |year=1976 |publisher=Ōgo Town Hall |ref=harv |editor=Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99955}} == External Links == * [https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1083 Ōgo Shrine (Maebashi Guide)] {{translated page|ja|大胡神社|version=103617795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大胡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438549}} [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Interchange|1=Komagata interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668353}} on the {{ill|Kita-Kantō Expressway|en|Kita-Kantō Expressway|WD=Q11404370|lt=Kitakantō Expressway|1=Kita-Kantō Expressway}} * 25-minute walk from {{ill|Ōgo Station|en|Ōgo Station|WD=Q11438553|lt=Ōgo Station|1=Ōgo Station}} on the Jōmō Electric Railway. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Ōgo Castle ruins|ja|大胡城址|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja|群馬県道16号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gumma Prefectural Route 16|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群馬県道16号大胡赤城線|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609753}} * {{ill|Arato River|ja|荒砥川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arato
