[[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] [[ja:大胡神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōgo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Oogo Jinja.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}} Kawarahama Town 615 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.42172222222222|N|139.15925|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} |ShrineRank = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) |Establishment = Unknown |Alternatenames = Kondo Daimyōjin |Festivals = Daidai Kagura |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma#Japan|label=Ōgo Shrine|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=18.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=09|lon_sec=33.3|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} |prefecture = {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} }} '''Ōgo Shrine''' (大胡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Historically, it served as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}} and was referred to as Kondo Shrine until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established through a partition from the {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the exact year of its foundation is unknown. During the deity's procession between {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} and Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Ōgo Shrine served as a resting point for the deity{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1593), {{ill|Ōgo Takashige|ja|大胡高繁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Takashige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the lord of Ōgo Castle, worshiped at the shrine. In 1589, Takashige issued a letter to the shrine priest of Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine, Nara Hara Kii-no-Kami, requesting a priest to perform the rituals for Kondo Shrine, which solidified the Nara family's role in maintaining the shrine{{Sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. The shrine was renamed Ōgo Shrine in 1909. That same year, multiple smaller shrines in the vicinity, such as Sugawara Shrine and Yasaka Shrines, were merged into Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. In 1943, the a11roach to the shrine was reoriented from the east to face south{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}}. Ōgo Shrine was designated as a Gōsha on April 26, 1944{{Cite web |title=Digital National Diet Library Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/2961687/1/3 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. == Structure and Features == The shrine features a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}} (dance stage) to the right of the worship hall, where kagura performances are offered annually. A monument dedicated to Okada Yoshimasa, the first popularly elected mayor of Ōgo and a distinguished Gunma Prefectural Assembly member, stands on the premises{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=433-435}}. == Deities Enshrined == * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * 43 additional deities * Eight auxiliary deities{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1062-1066}} == Festivals == === Daidai Kagura (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Maebashi City) === This ritual, performed on April 10, involves thirteen dances offered to pray for bountiful harvests. It is managed by the Daidai Kagura Preservation Society in the Ashigaru Town District{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/1/3/22442.html|title=Traditional Arts in Ōgo Area|publisher=Maebashi City|accessdate=2020-05-31}}. === Ōgo Gion Festival === Held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July, this festival originates from Yasaka Shrine, although the shrine receives its sacred wands from Ōgo Shrine{{sfn|Ōgo Town History Compilation Committee|1976|11=1032-1234}}. == Cultural Properties == === Designated Important Cultural Properties of Maebashi City === * '''Ōgo Shrine’s Mathematical Tablet (算額)''' - Donated in 1915 by Ohara Fukutarō, a student of shipwright Matake. This rare example of Meiji-period mathematics was designated on April 5, 1994{{Cite web |title=City-Designated Cultural Properties{{!}}Maebashi City |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5106.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-23}}. * '''Daidai Kagura''' - Designated as an intangible cultural property in 1969. * '''Muku Tree (無患子)''' - Estimated to be over 300 years old, it was designated in 2008{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yearly Cultural Reports |date=2008-12-12 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16128}}. == location == * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gumma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}} Kawarahama Town 615 == Access == * About 20 minutes from {{ill|Koma Interchange|ja|駒形インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma
